title: Eva inspired by Shin Megami Tensei rumour
display_title: Eva inspired by Shin Megami Tensei rumour
wikitext:
[]: Eva inspired by Shin Megami Tensei? (rumour)             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Apr 18, 2012 9:05 pm I heard a rumour that Anno loved SMT1 and SMT2, and that they gave him the idea to use religious symbols. Does anyone know anything about this?

[]:             Quote Post by Final Messenger » Wed Apr 18, 2012 9:38 pm that is intresting if it is true, I wonder if Mara http://megamitensei.wikia.com/wiki/Mara (warning possible NSFW) inspired him at all :3

[]:             Quote Post by Ceimoa Nan » Thu Apr 19, 2012 11:46 am On page 5 of Proposal of Evangelion , they note how Eva is supposed to feel like a video game. Specifically, they say "preparations to invade a discovered enemy stronghold would be done in the vein of a simulation or RPG game," but the only thing I can think of in the actual series corresponding to that would be Magmadiver. I noticed how video game-y the Tokyo-3 battles were, especially with the weapon delivery system, but I never noticed an RPG connection. Of course, this is talking about mechanics instead of religious symbolism, but it goes to show that they at least had video games in mind while making the series. Also, looking through that SMT wiki real quick, it looks like Lilith and Lilim are characters. I've never played the series, so it's news to me.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Apr 19, 2012 12:53 pm Megami Tensei tosses in symbolism/references from every religion and mythological tradition they can get their hands on, though, not just Judaism and Christianity. Anyway, I kinda doubt this, seeing as Anno does not appear to be much of a gamer and Megami Tensei was not exactly a mainstream sensation back in the day (as I understand it, just like in the US/EU the series didn't really get its breakout hit in Japan until Persona 3 ). My understanding is that Eva 's religious imagery and mythos were inspired at least in part by the worldview of the Aum Shinrikyo cult, though I don't know for sure whether this is true or just another rumor swirling around Eva 's production.

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Thu Apr 19, 2012 10:49 pm Ceimoa Nan wrote: Also, looking through that SMT wiki real quick, it looks like Lilith and Lilim are characters. I've never played the series, so it's news to me.
